mnemonics: Remember: User stories, Functional requirements, Objectives, and Scenarios define the Requirements Model
keyPoints: The requirements model bridges the gap between user needs and technical specifications.
It includes functional requirements (what the system does) and non-functional requirements (how well it does it).
Key artifacts are use cases, user stories, data models, and state diagrams.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on functional requirements while ignoring non-functional ones (like performance, security, usability).
Creating an overly complex or ambiguous model that is difficult for stakeholders to understand and validate.
